整套首页
unit_13 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_13

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 354 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 SE
Mortoris is dour and __________, seemingly incapable of smiling, let alone laughing.Mortoris is dour and __________, seemingly incapable of smiling, let alone laughing.
答案：E, F　lugubrious; gloomy
mirthfuljoviallet alone
mirthful: standard unit headword | jovial: standard unit headword | let alone: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　candor; antidote to; banal notion
biographyantidotecandor
biography: standard unit headword | antidote: standard unit headword | candor: standard unit headword
#3 TC DOUBLE
The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inadequate; secondhand
allegealleged
allege: standard unit headword | alleged: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
Like her literary heroine, George Eliot, Barbara Kingsolvers is an old-fashioned __________, deeply curious about all branches of human learning.Like her literary heroine, George Eliot, Barbara Kingsolvers is an old-fashioned __________, deeply curious about all branches of human learning.
答案：B　polymath
prodigycurious
prodigy: standard unit headword | curious: standard unit headword
#5 TC DOUBLE
The politician's record while in office, though (i) __________, hardly accounts for her high standing three decades later—a standing all the more (ii) __________ because of continuing assaults on her reputation during those years.The politician's record while in office, though (i) __________, hardly accounts for her high standing three decades later—a standing all the more (ii) __________ because of continuing assaults on her reputation during those years.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　admirable; unusual
standingall the more
standing: standard unit headword | all the more: standard unit headword
#6 SE
Even the most hard-nosed critics could hardly deny that the novelist’s best books are __________: they are filled with energy, imagination, and something close to a white-hot inspiration.Even the most hard-nosed critics could hardly deny that the novelist’s best books are __________: they are filled with energy, imagination, and something close to a white-hot inspiration.
答案：C, E　spirited; vigorous
vigorousspirited
vigorous: standard unit headword | spirited: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　validity; ingenious; eventually collapsed
luminousinsight
luminous: standard unit headword | insight: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
Even those observers who are the most __________ about genetic privacy issues would have to concede that genetic discrimination is rare: there have only been two cases of any notoriety.Even those observers who are the most __________ about genetic privacy issues would have to concede that genetic discrimination is rare: there have only been two cases of any notoriety.
答案：B　zealous
notorietycandid
notoriety: standard unit headword | candid: standard unit headword
#9 SE
Although men still dominate the ranks of full professors in the field of astronomy, the increasing numbers of younger women in the field could __________ a change in its gender mix.Although men still dominate the ranks of full professors in the field of astronomy, the increasing numbers of younger women in the field could __________ a change in its gender mix.
答案：D, E　presage; portend
alleviateportend
alleviate: standard unit headword | portend: standard unit headword

Earlier discussions with neighboring countries (i) __________ due to the government's failure to alter policies that those countries find objectionable. Moreover, there is every reason to (ii) __________ the success of further talks, since the government is, if anything, more intransigent.Earlier discussions with neighboring countries (i) __________ due to the government's failure to alter policies that those countries find objectionable. Moreover, there is every reason to (ii) __________ the success of further talks, since the government is, if anything, more intransigent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　foundered; doubt

Frequently that time happened in a great classroom somewhere, in the hands of the one (ii) __________ teacher we can each remember.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　exciting; consummate

The superintendent's subsequent vindication will have come as no surprise to them.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　credit; swayed by; probity

答案：C, D　assumed; counted on
monitorcountenance

Computer modeling narrows the range of uncertainties related to pollution impacts, uncertainties that have (ii) __________ environmental policy making on all levels.Environmental models are increasingly (i) __________ to the regulatory process. Computer modeling narrows the range of uncertainties related to pollution impacts, uncertainties that have (ii) __________ environmental policy making on all levels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　central; beset
inimicalperipheral
inimical: standard unit headword | peripheral: standard unit headword
#15 SE
The territory’s tradition of simple and low taxes, combined with a comparatively easygoing government, has earned it the __________ of its citizens and is widely seen as a main reason for its stunning rise to prosperity.The territory’s tradition of simple and low taxes, combined with a comparatively easygoing government, has earned it the __________ of its citizens and is widely seen as a main reason for its stunning rise to prosperity.
答案：E, F　approbation; adulation
adulationvituperation
adulation: standard unit headword | vituperation: standard unit headword

Though many avant-garde writers __________ traditional distinctions among literary categories, combining elements of biography and fiction, prose and poetry, this fusion of forms has been slow to catch on with publishers.Though many avant-garde writers __________ traditional distinctions among literary categories, combining elements of biography and fiction, prose and poetry, this fusion of forms has been slow to catch on with publishers.
答案：A　flout
biographyavant-garde

#17 TC TRIPLE
It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
autobiographycandor
autobiography: standard unit headword | candor: standard unit headword

The era’s examples of __________ that are cited by the author can be balanced in part by certain examples of dissent during the same period.The era’s examples of __________ that are cited by the author can be balanced in part by certain examples of dissent during the same period.
答案：B　authoritarianism
balanced
balanced: standard unit headword
#19 SE
Isaac Newton's career, which spanned nearly seven decades and continued to generate new results through the 1690s, was truly __________ and can therefore hardly be expected to exhibit a single continuous strand of development.Isaac Newton's career, which spanned nearly seven decades and continued to generate new results through the 1690s, was truly __________ and can therefore hardly be expected to exhibit a single continuous strand of development.
答案：B, F　multifarious; protean
proteanprodigious
protean: standard unit headword | prodigious: standard unit headword
#20 TC TRIPLE
Biographies tumble off the presses in profusion these days, a (i) __________ linked, for better or worse, to the current obsession with celebrity. Some critics complain that biographers too often (ii) __________ the messy reality of a life, and it's true that lesser authors simply glorify or, more often, vilify their subjects. But good biographers are far more respectful of (iii) __________: the best see not one monolithic truth about a person but many overlapping truths.Biographies tumble off the presses in profusion these days, a (i) __________ linked, for better or worse, to the current obsession with celebrity. Some critics complain that biographers too often (ii) __________ the messy reality of a life, and it's true that lesser authors simply glorify or, more often, vilify their subjects. But good biographers are far more respectful of (iii) __________: the best see not one monolithic truth about a person but many overlapping truths.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　boom; impose a specious coherence on; complexity
biographymonolithic
biography: standard unit headword | monolithic: standard unit headword

The idea of a “language instinct” may seem __________ to those who think of language as the zenith of the human intellect and of instincts as brute impulses.The idea of a “language instinct” may seem __________ to those who think of language as the zenith of the human intellect and of instincts as brute impulses.
答案：A　jarring
zenith
zenith: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　familiar; revisionist
revisionist

Scientific papers often (i)__________ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)__________ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)__________.Scientific papers often (i)__________ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)__________ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)__________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　misrepresent; efficiency; a comprehensive historical account
in the course of
in the course of: standard unit headword

In contrast to such sparsely populated terrestrial habitats as desert and tundra, the oceans __________ with a seemingly endless array of creatures.In contrast to such sparsely populated terrestrial habitats as desert and tundra, the oceans __________ with a seemingly endless array of creatures.
答案：A　teem
array
array: standard unit headword
#25 SE

答案：A, C　robust; strong
revision

答案：C　pastiche
pastiche

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　emulate; anachronistic
counterpart

Carving materials such as stone, bone, antler, wood, and ivory were (iii) __________, but paper and drawing tools were unknown until introduced by early explorers and missionaries.Inuit print making is less (i) __________ than carving in that it does not have substantial historical precedents, although there are (ii) __________ incised carvings on bone or antler, facial tattoo marks or inlay skin work on clothing, mitts and footwear. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　traditional; affinities with; available locally
facial
facial: standard unit headword
#29 SE
One of the vocalists who auditioned for a leading part in the local production of Sweeney Todd seemed to prefer __________ to any attempt at producing a melody: a more unpleasant voice was hard to imagine.One of the vocalists who auditioned for a leading part in the local production of Sweeney Todd seemed to prefer __________ to any attempt at producing a melody: a more unpleasant voice was hard to imagine.
答案：D, F　shrieking; caterwauling
caterwaul

The science of astronomy was begun by amateurs and today remains dependent on their contributions, which are incisive by virtue of being __________ by the a priori assumptions that often vitiate the work of professional research scientists.The science of astronomy was begun by amateurs and today remains dependent on their contributions, which are incisive by virtue of being __________ by the a priori assumptions that often vitiate the work of professional research scientists.
答案：B　unencumbered
incisive

At the heart of present-day studies on computational authorship attribution lies the hypothesis that each author has so personal a writing style that it can function as stylistic DNA. Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ every other authors’ writing style.At the heart of present-day studies on computational authorship attribution lies the hypothesis that each author has so personal a writing style that it can function as stylistic DNA. Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ every other authors’ writing style.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　stable; distinguishable from
authorship

But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
prying
prying: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic

There are no __________ criteria of excellence in art: works that once were ignored or even reviled now fetch millions of dollars at auction, while those that were most highly praised in their day now languish in storage.There are no __________ criteria of excellence in art: works that once were ignored or even reviled now fetch millions of dollars at auction, while those that were most highly praised in their day now languish in storage.
答案：D　immutable

(i)__________ was, in a way, his very theme. Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)__________ raw life? 答案：C, E　fairness; objectivity

Proving the forest-climate link through verifiable and experimental scientific means rather than observation was (ii)__________ for these individuals, a situation that eventually led to the link (iii)__________ justifications for forest conservation.

Blank 2
Blank 3

verifiable

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　favorable; lower at the periphery

答案：A　incontrovertible

But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)__________ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)__________ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering

答案：D　an adjunct to
remedy

#49 SE

答案：B, E　a hodgepodge; a patchwork

The more scientists study animal behavior and brain anatomy, however, the more it seems that very complex brains are (ii) __________ consciousness.Most scientists would argue that consciousness is (i) __________ animals with relatively complex brains. The more scientists study animal behavior and brain anatomy, however, the more it seems that very complex brains are (ii) __________ consciousness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　restricted to; unnecessary for
anatomy

#51 TC TRIPLE
In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　conceal the artifice; intentional; mimicking
spot
spot: standard unit headword
#52 SE
Despite her rather __________ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.Despite her rather __________ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.
答案：A, C　unconventional; quirky
quirky
quirky: standard unit headword
#53 TC SINGLE
The artists’ career was remarkable partly because it was so __________: she died, with only a few paintings to her credit, while still in her twenties.The artists’ career was remarkable partly because it was so __________: she died, with only a few paintings to her credit, while still in her twenties.
答案：E　abbreviated
felicitous
felicitous: standard unit headword
#54 TC TRIPLE
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
pitfall
pitfall: standard unit headword
#55 TC SINGLE
The science community’s perennial lament over inadequate budgets has come to seem increasingly __________, because government support for science and engineering has never been greater.The science community’s perennial lament over inadequate budgets has come to seem increasingly __________, because government support for science and engineering has never been greater.
答案：E　disingenuous
poignant
poignant: standard unit headword
#56 TC DOUBLE
For all of its (i) __________, there are a number of very substantial (ii) __________ that arise from the idea of the big bang. And many of these problems seem to get worse the further back in time we are willing to look.For all of its (i) __________, there are a number of very substantial (ii) __________ that arise from the idea of the big bang. And many of these problems seem to get worse the further back in time we are willing to look.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　verified predictions; puzzles
verify
verify: standard unit headword
#57 SE
Each of the country’s 26 states and most of its more than 2,000 municipalities have their own police forces, __________ communication and making it difficult to establish a coordinated law-enforcement strategy.Each of the country’s 26 states and most of its more than 2,000 municipalities have their own police forces, __________ communication and making it difficult to establish a coordinated law-enforcement strategy.
答案：B, C　impeding; hampering

#58 TC TRIPLE
Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Handi Murdd is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale’s creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy’s nonfiction.Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Handi Murdd is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale’s creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy’s nonfiction.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　a preference for; suppress; didacticism
incite
incite: standard unit headword
#59 SE
Despite a tendency to be overtly __________, the poetry does not consist solely of pious sentiments: it sparks the imagination and provides lively entertainment.Despite a tendency to be overtly __________, the poetry does not consist solely of pious sentiments: it sparks the imagination and provides lively entertainment.
答案：A, D　preachy; sanctimonious
preachy
preachy: standard unit headword

答案：B　obscure

anatomize: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　in eclipse; unsurpassed

A mere possibility argument such as "Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about" can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii) __________ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.Arguments based on mere possibilities (i) __________ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii) __________ public debates on technological options. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　epitomize; polarize; decisive

This (i)__________ is (ii)__________, as demonstrated at the conference by the many presentations that notably lacked an adequate methodology for ensuring that DNA was uncontaminated by material that is more recent. In fact, ancient DNA research presents extreme technical difficulties, in part precisely because of the (iii)__________ of surviving DNA.At a recent conference on ancient DNA, one presentation opened with the claim that the field was now mature and could move ahead with confidence. This (i)__________ is (ii)__________, as demonstrated at the conference by the many presentations that notably lacked an adequate methodology for ensuring that DNA was uncontaminated by material that is more recent. In fact, ancient DNA research presents extreme technical difficulties, in part precisely because of the (iii)__________ of surviving DNA.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　optimism; unfounded; corrupted nature

#82 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword

Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation's banking system is (i) __________ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　unencumbered by; discretionary

#95 TC TRIPLE
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)__________ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)__________: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)__________ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)__________ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)__________: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)__________ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for
insight
insight: standard unit headword
#96 TC DOUBLE
Fables often endure due to their (i) __________, often telling one simple narrative, based around one character. This is both by design, because direct statements are more easily remembered than florid ones, and by accident, as fables are passed from teller to teller, (ii) __________ details fall away, leaving only the essential story.Fables often endure due to their (i) __________, often telling one simple narrative, based around one character. This is both by design, because direct statements are more easily remembered than florid ones, and by accident, as fables are passed from teller to teller, (ii) __________ details fall away, leaving only the essential story.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　simplicity; superfluous

答案：B　sycophantic

incisive: standard unit headword
#106 TC DOUBLE
The novel's heroine shows a remarkable (i) __________ to worship at the altar of youth; in her world, youth is (ii) __________, while age, by contrast, confers competence and wisdom.The novel's heroine shows a remarkable (i) __________ to worship at the altar of youth; in her world, youth is (ii) __________, while age, by contrast, confers competence and wisdom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　disinclination; callow
callow
callow: standard unit headword
#107 TC TRIPLE
A bird’s feathers would seem to be a (i)__________ design for protecting a bird from attack by microscopic organisms. They create a warm, moist space next to the skin that could be an ideal incubator for spores. Wild birds rarely (ii)__________ skin diseases, however. The chemicals in the sebum include an array of antibacterial and anti-fungal agents that allow the bird’s skin to (iii)__________.A bird’s feathers would seem to be a (i)__________ design for protecting a bird from attack by microscopic organisms. They create a warm, moist space next to the skin that could be an ideal incubator for spores. Wild birds rarely (ii)__________ skin diseases, however. The chemicals in the sebum include an array of antibacterial and anti-fungal agents that allow the bird’s skin to (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　poor; contract; stay healthy
array
array: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
While early biographies of Florence Nightingale tended to be quite __________, Lytton Starchey’s irreverent 1918 passage about her ushered in a new era, making it acceptable, even fashionable, to criticize her.While early biographies of Florence Nightingale tended to be quite __________, Lytton Starchey’s irreverent 1918 passage about her ushered in a new era, making it acceptable, even fashionable, to criticize her.
答案：C　sycophantic
biography
biography: standard unit headword
#109 SE
The concept of the Hellenistic period in ancient history has proved useful but also __________, with scholars disagreeing on the dates when the period began and ended.The concept of the Hellenistic period in ancient history has proved useful but also __________, with scholars disagreeing on the dates when the period began and ended.
答案：A, B　slippery; elusive
elusive
elusive: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
Behavior economists found that the more (i)__________ options listed on the insurance make people all the more offish to endorse, partly because they hope to (ii)__________ some (iii)__________ in order to get a measure of peace of mind.Behavior economists found that the more (i)__________ options listed on the insurance make people all the more offish to endorse, partly because they hope to (ii)__________ some (iii)__________ in order to get a measure of peace of mind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; forgo; benefit
all the more
all the more: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　managing; solving

答案：E　a protean
protean
protean: standard unit headword

A cure for the common cold has been so elusive that it has become a modern symbol of __________.A cure for the common cold has been so elusive that it has become a modern symbol of __________.
答案：B, D　futility; pointless
elusive
elusive: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A, F, I　centralizing; raise doubts about; preclude

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　exhaustive; vivacity

答案：A　revisionist

答案：C, F　speculation; conjecture

#118 TC TRIPLE
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i) __________ life in the sea. One Maiacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii) __________: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) __________ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i) __________ life in the sea. One Maiacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii) __________: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) __________ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for
insight
insight: standard unit headword
#119 TC DOUBLE
Around the early 1980s, technology companies began creating the first portable machines that resemble today's laptop computers. Still, for many years, large and bulky desktop computers (i) __________ the market, because you always had to (ii) __________ something for portability. Compared with laptops, desktops had more power, bigger screens, more comfortable keyboards, and more storage space.Around the early 1980s, technology companies began creating the first portable machines that resemble today's laptop computers. Still, for many years, large and bulky desktop computers (i) __________ the market, because you always had to (ii) __________ something for portability. Compared with laptops, desktops had more power, bigger screens, more comfortable keyboards, and more storage space.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　held sway in; sacrifice
sway
sway: standard unit headword
#120 TC SINGLE
Considering each play individually limits the scope of Miller's conclusions; we are given many fine insights into particular plays, but necessarily we get very little __________.Considering each play individually limits the scope of Miller's conclusions; we are given many fine insights into particular plays, but necessarily we get very little __________.
答案：B　synthesis
insight
insight: standard unit headword
#121 SE
There are many insights in the essays collected in Observations on Modernity, but they are embedded in a dense English translation of a dense German original that may make many of them __________ to most readers.There are many insights in the essays collected in Observations on Modernity, but they are embedded in a dense English translation of a dense German original that may make many of them __________ to most readers.
答案：B, F　inaccessible; opaque
insight
insight: standard unit headword
#122 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
prying
prying: standard unit headword
#123 TC DOUBLE
Managers who categorically squelch insights from low-tiered employees run the obvious hazard of (i) __________ creativity; conversely, these very same managers are more likely to (ii) __________ any ideas that flow down from the top brass.Managers who categorically squelch insights from low-tiered employees run the obvious hazard of (i) __________ creativity; conversely, these very same managers are more likely to (ii) __________ any ideas that flow down from the top brass.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　smothering; unquestioningly embrace

insight: standard unit headword
#124 TC SINGLE
The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.
答案：A　perplexing
in the course of
in the course of: standard unit headword

In ways large and small, millions of people are taking active steps to __________ the medical mainstream, whether by taking herbal remedies for disease or by placing their hopes for a cure in alternative treatment.In ways large and small, millions of people are taking active steps to __________ the medical mainstream, whether by taking herbal remedies for disease or by placing their hopes for a cure in alternative treatment.
答案：B, E　sidestep; circumvent

One of the problems with recent books that perpetuate the view of Jane Austen as moral tutor, a sort of Miss Manners for the ages, is that they understand manners as (i)__________, as near-universal and timeless behavioral ideals or, worse still, a set of rules to be followed. It is crucial, however, to (ii)__________. In other words, one needs to resist (iii)__________ generic "manners" with more linguistically and historically precise designations, not least because Austen herself distinguishes.One of the problems with recent books that perpetuate the view of Jane Austen as moral tutor, a sort of Miss Manners for the ages, is that they understand manners as (i)__________, as near-universal and timeless behavioral ideals or, worse still, a set of rules to be followed. It is crucial, however, to (ii)__________. In other words, one needs to resist (iii)__________ generic "manners" with more linguistically and historically precise designations, not least because Austen herself distinguishes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　monolithic; differentiate various behaviors; conflating
monolithic

In the new biography, Gonzalez doesn't (i) __________ the aspects of her subject that have drawn criticism but instead creates them with considerable (ii) __________: hardly a fact or assertion goes by her without being sourced in the endnotes.In the new biography, Gonzalez doesn't (i) __________ the aspects of her subject that have drawn criticism but instead creates them with considerable (ii) __________: hardly a fact or assertion goes by her without being sourced in the endnotes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　evade; thoroughness

答案：C　derivative

答案：C, D　undermined; impaired

Scientific papers often (i)__________ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts because (ii)__________ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a scientific report and would be poorly served by (iii)__________.Scientific papers often (i)__________ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts because (ii)__________ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a scientific report and would be poorly served by (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　misrepresent; efficiency; a comprehensive historical account
in the course of
in the course of: standard unit headword
#131 TC DOUBLE
At the heart of present-day studies computational authorship attribution lies the hypothesis that each author has so personal a writing style that it can function as stylistic DNA. Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i) __________ and (ii) __________ every other author's writing style.At the heart of present-day studies computational authorship attribution lies the hypothesis that each author has so personal a writing style that it can function as stylistic DNA. Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i) __________ and (ii) __________ every other author's writing style.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　stable; distinguishable from
authorship

#132 SE
Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only __________ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only __________ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.
答案：A, E　prodigious; voracious
prodigious
prodigious: standard unit headword

答案：E　maudlin

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　overstate; panacea

答案：C, F　perennial; long-standing

#137 TC SINGLE
Readers are interested in Marx's life because of his ideas, but Sperber's book downplays his ideas to make room for his life, illustrating why intellectual biography can sometimes be a curiously __________ genre.Readers are interested in Marx's life because of his ideas, but Sperber's book downplays his ideas to make room for his life, illustrating why intellectual biography can sometimes be a curiously __________ genre.
答案：C　self-defeating
biography

#138 TC TRIPLE
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage
alleviate
alleviate: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　undermine; camouflage

答案：D　flummery

答案：E　intrinsic

答案：B, C　proclivity toward; predilection for

Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) __________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) __________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：D　boast of

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 1

#152 SE
The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.
答案：A, D　inimical to; adverse to

答案：E　unproven

His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)__________ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)__________ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.During the Harlem Renaissance, Alain Locke (i)__________ the first flourishing of a self-consciously racial art movement in America and was widely credited with providing the philosophical basis for its emergence. His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)__________ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)__________ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　presided over; undisputed; insufficient
insight
insight: standard unit headword

Galileo began to undermine humans' sense of self-importance a full five centuries ago, when he proved that Earth is not at the center of the universe, no matter how things look to us. Yet the tendency to judge the cosmos based on appearances and assumptions of our own (i) __________ has proved (ii) __________.Galileo began to undermine humans' sense of self-importance a full five centuries ago, when he proved that Earth is not at the center of the universe, no matter how things look to us. Yet the tendency to judge the cosmos based on appearances and assumptions of our own (i) __________ has proved (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　primacy; tenacious

#156 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong--and, consequently, we are lacking in any __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news--tales of how things went wrong--and, consequently, we are lacking in any __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：A, C　incisive; trenchant

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　preoccupied with; an indication of; evolves through

authorship: standard unit headword
#159 TC DOUBLE
It is often the case that a certain intellectual advantage may be found by being (i) __________ an established field of knowledge, but even if you accept the argument about the importance of (ii) __________ thinking, it is hardly the only significant thinking there is.It is often the case that a certain intellectual advantage may be found by being (i) __________ an established field of knowledge, but even if you accept the argument about the importance of (ii) __________ thinking, it is hardly the only significant thinking there is.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　on the periphery of; outsider
periphery
periphery: standard unit headword
#160 TC SINGLE
Many Latin American writers and critics have come to bristle at the very mention of the type of fiction termed “magic realism,” but to the common reader the appeal of such fiction is __________.Many Latin American writers and critics have come to bristle at the very mention of the type of fiction termed “magic realism,” but to the common reader the appeal of such fiction is __________.
答案：B　undiminished
elusive
elusive: standard unit headword
#161 SE
For certain economists, “pure” economic theory, that is, economic theory __________ a specific social structure, is impossible, much like a concept of anatomy that investigates no specific species.For certain economists, “pure” economic theory, that is, economic theory __________ a specific social structure, is impossible, much like a concept of anatomy that investigates no specific species.
答案：B, D　abstracted from; divorced from
anatomy
anatomy: standard unit headword
#162 TC TRIPLE
Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles
pastiche
pastiche: standard unit headword
#163 TC DOUBLE
Because self-defense was peripheral to the karate training techniques instituted throughout the Okinawan educational system in the early twentieth century, the pugilistic dimension of karate techniques was (i) __________ or even (ii) __________.Because self-defense was peripheral to the karate training techniques instituted throughout the Okinawan educational system in the early twentieth century, the pugilistic dimension of karate techniques was (i) __________ or even (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　intentionally downplayed; eliminated
peripheral
peripheral: standard unit headword
#164 TC SINGLE
It seems foolish to refuse the offer of an expedient that is both so __________ success and so difficult to create the absent.It seems foolish to refuse the offer of an expedient that is both so __________ success and so difficult to create the absent.
答案：D　indispensable to
inimical
inimical: standard unit headword
#165 SE
Rebecca West’s book Black Lamb and Grey Falcon is a singularly __________ achievement, 1,100 pages that meld the genres of travel narratives, autobiography, historical analysis, and philosophical meditation.Rebecca West’s book Black Lamb and Grey Falcon is a singularly __________ achievement, 1,100 pages that meld the genres of travel narratives, autobiography, historical analysis, and philosophical meditation.
答案：C, E　polymathic; encyclopedic
autobiography
autobiography: standard unit headword

The label "melodrama" tends to have (i) __________ implications when used to describe a film with historical subject matter. Melodrama is widely perceived as antithetical to cinematic realism and thus, from a perspective that seeks (ii) __________ rendering of the historical past, incapable of delivering the necessary (iii) __________.The label "melodrama" tends to have (i) __________ implications when used to describe a film with historical subject matter. Melodrama is widely perceived as antithetical to cinematic realism and thus, from a perspective that seeks (ii) __________ rendering of the historical past, incapable of delivering the necessary (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　pejorative; a seemingly unmediated; objectivity
unmediated
unmediated: standard unit headword
#167 TC DOUBLE
Humans have a curious (i) __________ problems in terms of (ii) __________, and scientists are no exception. For example, the principal challenge to geological uniformitarianism has been its logical opposite - catastrophism.Humans have a curious (i) __________ problems in terms of (ii) __________, and scientists are no exception. For example, the principal challenge to geological uniformitarianism has been its logical opposite - catastrophism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predilection to define; dichotomies
curious
curious: standard unit headword
#168 TC SINGLE
Not only was Jarry __________ the pandemonium his play sparkled, he actually worked to foment the conflict, organizing a posse to boo if the rest of the audience applauded and cheer if the other attendees booed.Not only was Jarry __________ the pandemonium his play sparkled, he actually worked to foment the conflict, organizing a posse to boo if the rest of the audience applauded and cheer if the other attendees booed.
答案：C　unperturbed by
detached
detached: standard unit headword
#169 SE
During sleep the brain-wave patterns of dogs are similar to people's, and they exhibit the stages of electrical activity that are observed in humans, so there is nothing there to __________ the notion that dogs dream.During sleep the brain-wave patterns of dogs are similar to people's, and they exhibit the stages of electrical activity that are observed in humans, so there is nothing there to __________ the notion that dogs dream.
答案：C, F　undermine; contradict
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#170 TC TRIPLE
Having an intense and long-standing culture of (i)__________, the company understandably has a reputation as the supreme corporate (ii)__________. But even by these standards, the degree of secrecy surrounding the company's most recent research is (iii)__________.Having an intense and long-standing culture of (i)__________, the company understandably has a reputation as the supreme corporate (ii)__________. But even by these standards, the degree of secrecy surrounding the company's most recent research is (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　privacy; enigma; remarkable
standing
standing: standard unit headword
#171 TC DOUBLE
After a century that saw age-old notions of artistic (i) __________ cast to the winds, it seems curious that some critics quaintly continue to condemn works of art for being "bad," as though the meaning of the term had (ii) __________.After a century that saw age-old notions of artistic (i) __________ cast to the winds, it seems curious that some critics quaintly continue to condemn works of art for being "bad," as though the meaning of the term had (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　quality; remained constant

curious: standard unit headword
#172 SE
Some research suggests that praising children's talents and abilities may __________ their confidence, making them reluctant to tackle more difficult challenges that carry a greater risk of failure.Some research suggests that praising children's talents and abilities may __________ their confidence, making them reluctant to tackle more difficult challenges that carry a greater risk of failure.
答案：B, F　undermine; sap
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#173 TC SINGLE
One might expect someone of such __________ views to have a comparably dour personality, but people who have worked with her talk about how she uses her considerable charm to convince people that she is right.One might expect someone of such __________ views to have a comparably dour personality, but people who have worked with her talk about how she uses her considerable charm to convince people that she is right.
答案：D　astringent
felicitous
felicitous: standard unit headword
#174 TC TRIPLE
Overall, fish farming may provide a (i)__________ wild fish stocks, but for certain species, the farmers cause a net loss of wild fish. Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. Such (ii)__________ feed plus other environmental side effects undermine the expected (iii)__________ fish farming.Overall, fish farming may provide a (i)__________ wild fish stocks, but for certain species, the farmers cause a net loss of wild fish. Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. Such (ii)__________ feed plus other environmental side effects undermine the expected (iii)__________ fish farming.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　supplement to; appetite for; benefits of
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#175 TC DOUBLE
Fallows argues that America's two-and-a-half centuries of rapid economic growth, rather than portending endless, similarly paced future advances, have amounted to harvesting the "low-hanging fruit" of open land, cheap energy, and industrial-era breakthroughs. Thus, such growth has been (i) __________ episode that is unlikely to be (ii) __________.Fallows argues that America's two-and-a-half centuries of rapid economic growth, rather than portending endless, similarly paced future advances, have amounted to harvesting the "low-hanging fruit" of open land, cheap energy, and industrial-era breakthroughs. Thus, such growth has been (i) __________ episode that is unlikely to be (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　a unique; sustained
portend
portend: standard unit headword

The diversity of uses of the term "emotional intelligence" in popular culture has (i) __________ definitional clarity: the term has become so (ii) __________ that it is (iii) __________ conceptual meaning.The diversity of uses of the term "emotional intelligence" in popular culture has (i) __________ definitional clarity: the term has become so (ii) __________ that it is (iii) __________ conceptual meaning.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　impaired; protean; nearly bereft of

答案：C　excoriated

答案：B, F　obtain; glean

Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii)__________.It would be naïve to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer’s true state of mind, since the (i)__________ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Blank 2
答案：B, F　penalty; balanced reflection

答案：E　germane to

#181 SE

答案：D, F　paucity; dearth

#182 TC TRIPLE
Indira Gandhi is independent India's most puzzling political figure. She was enigmatic and often (i) __________ in person, and her political identity was no less (ii) __________. As Prime Minister, she seemed to be a woman of great (iii) __________, but privately she was diffident and self-doubting.Indira Gandhi is independent India's most puzzling political figure. She was enigmatic and often (i) __________ in person, and her political identity was no less (ii) __________. As Prime Minister, she seemed to be a woman of great (iii) __________, but privately she was diffident and self-doubting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　opaque; elusive; self-assurance

elusive: standard unit headword
#183 TC DOUBLE
Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.Many nineteenth-century women writers in the United States encountered fierce criticism for stepping beyond what society considered their designated sphere. Early in her career as an author, Lydia Maria Child sought to (i) __________ such (ii) __________ by keeping her authorship secret.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　evade; opprobrium
authorship
authorship: standard unit headword
#184 TC SINGLE
The connection between Alan Turing's prewar mathematical ideas and the first stored-program electronic computers of the mid-1940s may be __________ than assumed: Turing's reflections were never intended as blueprints for the construction of physical automatic machines.The connection between Alan Turing's prewar mathematical ideas and the first stored-program electronic computers of the mid-1940s may be __________ than assumed: Turing's reflections were never intended as blueprints for the construction of physical automatic machines.
答案：B　more tenuous
mediate
mediate: standard unit headword
#185 SE
As a historical genre, biography is best when __________, a careful reconstruction of the past in all its unfamiliar particularity.As a historical genre, biography is best when __________, a careful reconstruction of the past in all its unfamiliar particularity.
答案：E, F　meticulous; thorough
biography
biography: standard unit headword
#186 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
monolithic
monolithic: standard unit headword
#187 TC DOUBLE
The enormous three-volume biographical work, which has taken the author 28 years to complete, serves to caution one about the (i) __________ of literary biography when it becomes an obsessive and all-consuming project, a (ii) __________ attempt to relive the subject's life vicariously.The enormous three-volume biographical work, which has taken the author 28 years to complete, serves to caution one about the (i) __________ of literary biography when it becomes an obsessive and all-consuming project, a (ii) __________ attempt to relive the subject's life vicariously.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　perils; doomed

答案：B　violations

#189 SE
Margaret Oliphant's literary output was __________: it included almost 100 novels, 50 short stories, 25 nonfictional works, and in the region of 400 articles published in the periodical press.Margaret Oliphant's literary output was __________: it included almost 100 novels, 50 short stories, 25 nonfictional works, and in the region of 400 articles published in the periodical press.
答案：C, D　ample; prodigious

But while (i)__________ claims are made for these treatments, the scientific evidence for them often (ii)__________: studies and clinical trials, when they exist at all, can be (iii)__________ in design and too small to yield reliable insights.An estimated 80 million adults in the United States use some form of alternative medicine, from herbs and megavitamins to yoga and acupuncture. But while (i)__________ claims are made for these treatments, the scientific evidence for them often (ii)__________: studies and clinical trials, when they exist at all, can be (iii)__________ in design and too small to yield reliable insights.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　sweeping; lags far behind; shoddy

Moreover, high oil prices had themselves started to act as a short-term (ii)__________ the global economy, thus further dampening demand.World demand for oil had been intensified, but it slackened because China’s surge in oil consumption had (i)__________. Moreover, high oil prices had themselves started to act as a short-term (ii)__________ the global economy, thus further dampening demand.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　slowed; drag on

commence: standard unit headword
#192 SE

答案：B, D　companionable; gregarious

Some people might object to pairing art and physics, since art is considered to be relatively __________ and physics, until recently, scrupulously avoided any mention of the inner thoughts that related to the outer world.Some people might object to pairing art and physics, since art is considered to be relatively __________ and physics, until recently, scrupulously avoided any mention of the inner thoughts that related to the outer world.
答案：B　subjective

There is nothing (ii)__________ about the new location: the building's design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)__________ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)__________ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)__________ about the new location: the building's design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)__________ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　an unprepossessing; spare; a grandiose

Economists, for example, have peddled their theories as a way of gaining public prominence or political appointment, while politicians have (ii)__________ economic doctrines as possible solutions to the nation’s social problems.

答案：E　a bequest

Such metaphors may (ii) __________ adversarial conceptions of argument, leading interlocutors to behave aggressively in verbal disputes, thereby (iii) __________ a process that ideally leads to resolution.

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：E　measured

答案：C, E　an itinerant; a peripatetic

Because many island herbivores, such as the tortoises of the Galapagos Islands and the flightless geese of Hawaii, are (ii) __________, arboreal foliage is (iii) __________ to them.One reason island trees may not have evolved defenses against herbivores is that such defenses may be (i) __________. Because many island herbivores, such as the tortoises of the Galapagos Islands and the flightless geese of Hawaii, are (ii) __________, arboreal foliage is (iii) __________ to them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　superfluous; earthbound; inaccessible

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　a privileged; a disproportionate

答案：D　equanimity

答案：D, E　a prodigious; an immense

We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.Genetic researchers have recently suggested that our political leanings may be determined by our DNA, a view that tends to undermine our reflective self-flattery. We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　revel in; deliberative; defies

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　laboratory; tried

#212 TC TRIPLE
Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)__________ the (ii)__________ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)__________, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)__________ the (ii)__________ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)__________, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　struck by; suffocating lack of creative freedom; monitored
biographymonitor
biography: standard unit headword | monitor: standard unit headword
#213 TC SINGLE
The law student found traditional legal reference works unhelpful, since such works assume from the reader a considerable familiarity with the intricacies of contract law, an assumption that was, in his case at least, __________.The law student found traditional legal reference works unhelpful, since such works assume from the reader a considerable familiarity with the intricacies of contract law, an assumption that was, in his case at least, __________.
答案：B　unwarranted
incisive
incisive: standard unit headword
#214 SE
Some social scientists who begin their studies of small communities with the intention of remaining detached revise their initial approach on discovering that personal involvement with subjects can actually __________ the gathering of important data.Some social scientists who begin their studies of small communities with the intention of remaining detached revise their initial approach on discovering that personal involvement with subjects can actually __________ the gathering of important data.
答案：B, C　expedite; facilitate
detached
detached: standard unit headword
#215 TC DOUBLE
World demand for oil had been intensifying, but it slackened because China's surge in oil consumption had (i) __________. Moreover, high oil prices had themselves started to act as a short-term (ii) __________ the global economy, thus further dampening demand.World demand for oil had been intensifying, but it slackened because China's surge in oil consumption had (i) __________. Moreover, high oil prices had themselves started to act as a short-term (ii) __________ the global economy, thus further dampening demand.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　slowed; drag on
commence
commence: standard unit headword
#216 SE
Tompkinson’s prior donations to the university, while very generous, failed to __________ the magnitude of her latest gift.Tompkinson’s prior donations to the university, while very generous, failed to __________ the magnitude of her latest gift.
答案：B, D　portend; predict
portendundermine
portend: standard unit headword | undermine: standard unit headword

The __________ of the event organizers' promise of lavish accommodations was revealed when guests arrived to find themselves assigned to quarters that barely outstripped a campground.The __________ of the event organizers' promise of lavish accommodations was revealed when guests arrived to find themselves assigned to quarters that barely outstripped a campground.
答案：C　mendacity
candor
candor: standard unit headword
#218 TC TRIPLE
For feminist scholars, Kessler-Harris's concern that the term "labor history" (i)__________ images of male workers exclusively may be (ii)__________; her own immense contributions to the field offer evidence that feminism has (iii)__________ the questions posed by labor historians, moving women's concerns from the periphery closer to the center of labor history writing.For feminist scholars, Kessler-Harris's concern that the term "labor history" (i)__________ images of male workers exclusively may be (ii)__________; her own immense contributions to the field offer evidence that feminism has (iii)__________ the questions posed by labor historians, moving women's concerns from the periphery closer to the center of labor history writing.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　evokes; too pessimistic; altered
periphery
periphery: standard unit headword
#219 TC DOUBLE
By dramatizing the lives of her subjects with candor, sympathy, and a healthy strain of skepticism, director Esther Bell offers an (i) __________ the whimsy and solipsism (ii) __________ to much of what passes for independent filmmaking.By dramatizing the lives of her subjects with candor, sympathy, and a healthy strain of skepticism, director Esther Bell offers an (i) __________ the whimsy and solipsism (ii) __________ to much of what passes for independent filmmaking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　antidote to; endemic
antidotecandor
antidote: standard unit headword | candor: standard unit headword
#220 SE
The action in Zadie Smith’s novel On Beauty is mediated by an unabashedly __________ narrator who does not hesitate to inform us, as once upon a time the narrators of novels were wont to do, how we behave in general and how society usually works.The action in Zadie Smith’s novel On Beauty is mediated by an unabashedly __________ narrator who does not hesitate to inform us, as once upon a time the narrators of novels were wont to do, how we behave in general and how society usually works.
答案：B, C　obtrusive; conspicuous
mediate
mediate: standard unit headword
#221 TC TRIPLE
Victories in intellectual battles in the human sciences are rarely (i) __________. While factionalism may persist, (ii) __________ also tend to occur, as reigning paradigms selectively (iii) __________ those revisionist contributions that offer greater analytical comprehension or enrich our range of empirical reference.Victories in intellectual battles in the human sciences are rarely (i) __________. While factionalism may persist, (ii) __________ also tend to occur, as reigning paradigms selectively (iii) __________ those revisionist contributions that offer greater analytical comprehension or enrich our range of empirical reference.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　total; accommodations; incorporate

#222 TC TRIPLE
The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to
antidotein the course of
antidote: standard unit headword | in the course of: standard unit headword

Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i) __________ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii) __________ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　an anonymous; confessional

spirited: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　scarcely; hardly

The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i) __________. Or rather, it is—you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum—but it also refers to (ii) __________, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i) __________. Or rather, it is—you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum—but it also refers to (ii) __________, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　metaphor; a real biological feature

elusive: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　evasive; elusive

答案：B, F, I　concision; sprawling; economical

The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)__________, a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)__________ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)__________ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)__________, a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)__________ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)__________ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deferential; radical; profound shift

There is a powerful tradition within French writing that has (i)__________ the bounds of conventional morality, (ii)__________ the dictates of Enlightenment reason, and expressed (iii)__________ violence. It is as if the culture that, perhaps more strongly than any other, celebrated reason and geometrical order, also provoked within itself a deep, wild, and willfully primitive reaction, a return of the repressed par excellence.There is a powerful tradition within French writing that has (i)__________ the bounds of conventional morality, (ii)__________ the dictates of Enlightenment reason, and expressed (iii)__________ violence. It is as if the culture that, perhaps more strongly than any other, celebrated reason and geometrical order, also provoked within itself a deep, wild, and willfully primitive reaction, a return of the repressed par excellence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　challenged; defied; an abiding fascination with

excellence: standard unit headword

Aside from nutrient availability, growth rate is the primary determinant of size in single-celled organisms: rapidly growing Escherichia coli cells are more than twice as large as their __________ counterparts.Aside from nutrient availability, growth rate is the primary determinant of size in single-celled organisms: rapidly growing Escherichia coli cells are more than twice as large as their __________ counterparts.
答案：C, E　more sluggish; slower
