One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)__________. This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)__________, even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)__________. Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)__________. This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)__________, even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)__________. Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　isolated; negligible; cause for concern
luminous
luminous: standard unit headword

Although anecdotes about her abound, it is difficult to verify facts about al-Khansa'--like many early poetic figures, she may well be a composite figure, her corpus __________ the works of many women poets.Although anecdotes about her abound, it is difficult to verify facts about al-Khansa'--like many early poetic figures, she may well be a composite figure, her corpus __________ the works of many women poets.
答案：B, F　an amalgamation of; a fusion of

Blank 2

答案：B, E　finer; more sensitive

The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　difficulty of; still more acute; specious

If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) __________, she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) __________, she counseled caution.Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) __________ over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. 答案：A, D, H　consistent; espoused; insufficient

While organic architects may seem to be pushing the boundaries of spatial possibilities further than most of the architectural world is, their work actually shows (i) __________ symmetry, geometric modules, and ordering devices that can appear (ii) __________ when compared with so-called deconstructivist work, which has held the avant-garde position in architecture since the 1980s and which (iii) __________ the computer-aided possibility of building forms that are not normally found in nature or the built environment.While organic architects may seem to be pushing the boundaries of spatial possibilities further than most of the architectural world is, their work actually shows (i) __________ symmetry, geometric modules, and ordering devices that can appear (ii) __________ when compared with so-called deconstructivist work, which has held the avant-garde position in architecture since the 1980s and which (iii) __________ the computer-aided possibility of building forms that are not normally found in nature or the built environment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　an adherence to; tame; embraces

Blank 2

Whether (ii) __________ a long-standing idea or showing disdain for received political wisdom, the scientific ethic, which demands that the scientist follow the evidence wherever it leads, is a threat to (iii) __________ of all kinds.Science is inherently (i) __________. Whether (ii) __________ a long-standing idea or showing disdain for received political wisdom, the scientific ethic, which demands that the scientist follow the evidence wherever it leads, is a threat to (iii) __________ of all kinds.
Blank 1
Blank 2

#258 TC DOUBLE
The enlightenment philosophers of the eighteenth century acknowledged that unrestricted freedom to publish could (i) __________ motivated by envy or hatred, but they hoped that any such (ii) __________ enabled by expanded liberties would suffer from being publicly exposed.The enlightenment philosophers of the eighteenth century acknowledged that unrestricted freedom to publish could (i) __________ motivated by envy or hatred, but they hoped that any such (ii) __________ enabled by expanded liberties would suffer from being publicly exposed.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　facilitate calumny; defamation

#259 SE
As a long-standing destination for Asian, European, and African traders, nineteenth-century Zanzibar was __________ place; hence the proverbial claim that a flute played in Zanzibar sets many nations dancing.As a long-standing destination for Asian, European, and African traders, nineteenth-century Zanzibar was __________ place; hence the proverbial claim that a flute played in Zanzibar sets many nations dancing.
答案：B, C　a cosmopolitan; an inclusive

standing: standard unit headword
#260 TC TRIPLE
Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　deflects; untrue; gauge its veracity
autobiography
autobiography: standard unit headword
#261 TC DOUBLE
Although the first exoplanet discovered was found to be orbiting too close to its star to support life, scientists quickly discovered exoplanets in more (i) __________ orbits, making the detection of potentially habitable exoplanets seem (ii) __________.Although the first exoplanet discovered was found to be orbiting too close to its star to support life, scientists quickly discovered exoplanets in more (i) __________ orbits, making the detection of potentially habitable exoplanets seem (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　clement; likely
superfluous
superfluous: standard unit headword
#262 SE
Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.
答案：C, E　a derisive; a contemptuous
autobiography
autobiography: standard unit headword
#263 TC TRIPLE
Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.Many writers are (i) __________ the term "unauthorized biography" because it sounds so (ii) __________. Admittedly, biography by its very nature is (iii) __________ a life--an intimate and possibly unflattering examination by the biographer, who burrows deeper and deeper to reveal the unseen and illuminate the unexpected--but unauthorized biographies can be a public service and a boon to future historians.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　uncomfortable with; nefarious; an invasion of
biography
biography: standard unit headword
#264 TC DOUBLE
While writers on Parker often present her as an avaricious stick figure, thus making her an object of (i) __________, Nash, by contrast, gives a more (ii) __________ and sympathetic portrait of the woman.While writers on Parker often present her as an avaricious stick figure, thus making her an object of (i) __________, Nash, by contrast, gives a more (ii) __________ and sympathetic portrait of the woman.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　opprobrium; nuanced
adulation
adulation: standard unit headword
#265 SE
Edric's imagination has always been drawn to the peripheral, to characters who are set apart or seeking a geography to match their sense of spiritual __________.Edric's imagination has always been drawn to the peripheral, to characters who are set apart or seeking a geography to match their sense of spiritual __________.
答案：A, B　exile; separation
peripheral
peripheral: standard unit headword
#266 TC TRIPLE
The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　inherit; tend to embody; resist
standing
standing: standard unit headword
#267 TC DOUBLE
Although weeds often (i) __________ and are (ii) __________ human activities, their cussedness and refusal to play by human rules makes them subversive and the very essence of wildness.Although weeds often (i) __________ and are (ii) __________ human activities, their cussedness and refusal to play by human rules makes them subversive and the very essence of wildness.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　follow; dependent on
thwart
thwart: standard unit headword
#268 SE
In person, Downing is generally rather __________, seemingly incapable of smiling, let alone laughing.In person, Downing is generally rather __________, seemingly incapable of smiling, let alone laughing.
答案：C, D　dolorous; lugubrious
let alone
let alone: standard unit headword
#269 TC TRIPLE
The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　anonymity; unable to establish; dismiss
notoriety
notoriety: standard unit headword
#270 TC DOUBLE
She is (i) __________ on the Senate floor, just as she is elsewhere: her speech is (ii) __________ with the verbal pomposity of much senatorial oratory.She is (i) __________ on the Senate floor, just as she is elsewhere: her speech is (ii) __________ with the verbal pomposity of much senatorial oratory.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　plainspoken; unadorned
gregarious
gregarious: standard unit headword
#271 SE
According to Edelstein, republicanism was a __________ movement in eighteenth-century France, though not nearly as peripheral as has sometimes been claimed.According to Edelstein, republicanism was a __________ movement in eighteenth-century France, though not nearly as peripheral as has sometimes been claimed.
答案：D, F　fringe; marginal
peripheral
peripheral: standard unit headword
#272 TC TRIPLE
Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.Even mild criticism of public radio news reporting seems (i) __________, because the (ii) __________ such programming regularly receives is well deserved: the reporting is (iii) __________. In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　churlish; approbation; consistently perspicacious
excellence
excellence: standard unit headword
#273 TC DOUBLE
By using (i) __________ prose throughout, the contributors to this anthology have been able to make their subject seem (ii) __________ in the extreme--of concern only to one another.By using (i) __________ prose throughout, the contributors to this anthology have been able to make their subject seem (ii) __________ in the extreme--of concern only to one another.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　esoteric; parochial
luminous
luminous: standard unit headword
#274 SE
Believing fervently that very few truly good poems are written without a great deal of deliberation and revision, the poet urged her creative-writing students to be extremely __________.Believing fervently that very few truly good poems are written without a great deal of deliberation and revision, the poet urged her creative-writing students to be extremely __________.
答案：B, D　fastidious; finicky
revision
revision: standard unit headword
#275 TC TRIPLE
Although the impression that quantum mechanics is limited to the microworld (i) __________ the public understanding of science, it is (ii) __________. Quantum effects may be harder to see in the macroworld, but experiments have now confirmed that these effects are (iii) __________ than anyone ever suspected.Although the impression that quantum mechanics is limited to the microworld (i) __________ the public understanding of science, it is (ii) __________. Quantum effects may be harder to see in the macroworld, but experiments have now confirmed that these effects are (iii) __________ than anyone ever suspected.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　permeates; erroneous; more pervasive
undermineelusive
undermine: standard unit headword | elusive: standard unit headword
#276 TC DOUBLE
Though extremely (i) __________ about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) __________ and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next.Though extremely (i) __________ about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) __________ and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　reticent; discretion
candid
candid: standard unit headword
#277 SE
Upon taking office as president, he became __________ of a vigorous and energetic government, a stance that seemed at odds with earlier statements of his views, in which he had advocated strictly limited national government.Upon taking office as president, he became __________ of a vigorous and energetic government, a stance that seemed at odds with earlier statements of his views, in which he had advocated strictly limited national government.
答案：B, D　an exponent; a champion
vigorous
vigorous: standard unit headword
#278 TC TRIPLE
Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)__________ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)__________ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)__________ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)__________ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)__________ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)__________ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#279 TC DOUBLE
The study of the evolutionary interaction between herbivorous dinosaurs and the plants they fed on, as reflected in the fossil record, (i) __________. For this reason, many of the interpretations the author presents in her recent book on the subject are undoubtedly (ii) __________.The study of the evolutionary interaction between herbivorous dinosaurs and the plants they fed on, as reflected in the fossil record, (i) __________. For this reason, many of the interpretations the author presents in her recent book on the subject are undoubtedly (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　is in its infancy; provisional
superfluous
superfluous: standard unit headword
#280 SE
They applaud the musicals of the 1930s and 1940s, whose plethora of stars, jokes, dances, witty dialogue, and general gaiety make today’s offerings seem __________ by comparison.They applaud the musicals of the 1930s and 1940s, whose plethora of stars, jokes, dances, witty dialogue, and general gaiety make today’s offerings seem __________ by comparison.
答案：A, E　cheerless; gloomy
mirthfuljovial
mirthful: standard unit headword | jovial: standard unit headword
#281 TC TRIPLE
The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine
elusive
elusive: standard unit headword
#282 TC DOUBLE
The superficial agreement that characterizes the books' accounts of the incident is (i) __________, since it obscures the profound (ii) __________ that exist in their authors' understandings of the incident's significance.The superficial agreement that characterizes the books' accounts of the incident is (i) __________, since it obscures the profound (ii) __________ that exist in their authors' understandings of the incident's significance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　misleading; differences
insight
insight: standard unit headword
#283 SE
When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that __________ logic.When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that __________ logic.
答案：E, F　defied; disregarded
sway
sway: standard unit headword
#284 TC TRIPLE
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky’s description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; apt; impugned
countenance
countenance: standard unit headword
#285 TC DOUBLE
Those who knew the senator well were convinced that in spite of his (i) __________ in larger political matters, there was no (ii) __________ in his expressions of feeling for his constituents.Those who knew the senator well were convinced that in spite of his (i) __________ in larger political matters, there was no (ii) __________ in his expressions of feeling for his constituents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　duplicity; dissemblance
candor
candor: standard unit headword
#286 SE
Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the __________ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the __________ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous
mediocre
mediocre: standard unit headword
#287 TC TRIPLE
Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)__________ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)__________. Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)__________ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)__________. Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　supplanted; an act of private charity; absence
civic
civic: standard unit headword
#288 TC DOUBLE
Because the requerimiento, a formal statement in Spanish that was read to indigenous people in Southwestern pueblos during the Coronado expedition in the 1540s, was replete with concepts and terms that (i) __________ the pueblo world of that day, its audience likely found it (ii) __________.Because the requerimiento, a formal statement in Spanish that was read to indigenous people in Southwestern pueblos during the Coronado expedition in the 1540s, was replete with concepts and terms that (i) __________ the pueblo world of that day, its audience likely found it (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　lacked equivalents in; inscrutable
portend
portend: standard unit headword
#289 SE
Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could __________ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.Advocates for workers’ right have adopted a new strategy, one that will require considerable ingenuity but that, if successful, could __________ a movement aimed at making labor rights an unassailable feature of American democracy.
答案：B, E　galvanize; animate
thwart
thwart: standard unit headword
#290 TC TRIPLE
Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#291 TC DOUBLE
Despite Freud's admitted agnosticism about whether consciousness has a neurological basis, the question was (i) __________ to his model of the mind. Indeed, placing consciousness in a general neurophysiological schema of the mind (ii) __________ Freud's entire conceptual understanding of the psyche.Despite Freud's admitted agnosticism about whether consciousness has a neurological basis, the question was (i) __________ to his model of the mind. Indeed, placing consciousness in a general neurophysiological schema of the mind (ii) __________ Freud's entire conceptual understanding of the psyche.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　critical; oriented
inimical
inimical: standard unit headword
#292 SE
Apparent flaws in the sculptor’s work have not __________ its respectful reception by most modern critics.Apparent flaws in the sculptor’s work have not __________ its respectful reception by most modern critics.
答案：D, F　prevented; precluded
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#293 TC TRIPLE
Publishers, publicists, and broadcasters love anniversaries, those occasions when historical events become (i)__________ in (ii)__________ culture of celebration. On such occasions patriotic sentiment and national pride wrapped in the panoply of history to manufacture a mythical past that is serviceable for public (iii)__________.Publishers, publicists, and broadcasters love anniversaries, those occasions when historical events become (i)__________ in (ii)__________ culture of celebration. On such occasions patriotic sentiment and national pride wrapped in the panoply of history to manufacture a mythical past that is serviceable for public (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　marketable artifacts; a commercial; consumption

elusive: standard unit headword

Originality of artistic subject matter was never (i) __________ in ancient Egypt--in fact, it was generally (ii) __________; as a result, the artist's ambition was concentrated solely on thoroughness and precision of execution.Originality of artistic subject matter was never (i) __________ in ancient Egypt--in fact, it was generally (ii) __________; as a result, the artist's ambition was concentrated solely on thoroughness and precision of execution.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　prized; proscribed
monitor
monitor: standard unit headword

The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would __________ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would __________ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.
答案：A, B　combine; meld

On such occasions patriotic sentiment and national pride are wrapped in the panoply of history to manufacture a mythical past that is serviceable for public (iii)__________.

答案：B, E　waning; ebbing

Given children's active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as (i) __________ virtue in young children, but it turns out that lying is the more (ii) __________ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii) __________ cognitive development and social skills in a way that honesty simply does not.Given children's active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as (i) __________ virtue in young children, but it turns out that lying is the more (ii) __________ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii) __________ cognitive development and social skills in a way that honesty simply does not.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands

Although Wynne claims to recognize that __________ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.Although Wynne claims to recognize that __________ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.
答案：C, F　insufficient; meager

Anna Gavalda's fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for Me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda's fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for Me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain

Even manifest flaws in the sculptor’s work have not __________ its respectful reception by most modern critics.Even manifest flaws in the sculptor’s work have not __________ its respectful reception by most modern critics.
答案：D, F　prevented; precluded

#302 TC TRIPLE
A certain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i) __________. After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii) __________ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii) __________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i) __________. After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii) __________ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii) __________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
pitfall
pitfall: standard unit headword
#303 SE
Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to __________ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.Individuals, governments, and companies show ample ability to __________ themselves by setting goals based on current conditions and then blindly following them even when those conditions change drastically.
答案：A, E　hamstring; impair
invigorate
invigorate: standard unit headword
#304 TC TRIPLE
Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at
alleviate
alleviate: standard unit headword
#305 SE
According to some political analysts, the candidate’s occasionally rambling responses to questions suggest that she has been out of circulation for a while and her debating skills need to be __________.According to some political analysts, the candidate’s occasionally rambling responses to questions suggest that she has been out of circulation for a while and her debating skills need to be __________.
答案：A, C　honed; enhanced
remedy
remedy: standard unit headword
#306 TC TRIPLE
Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Hadji Murad is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale's creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy's nonfiction.Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Hadji Murad is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale's creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy's nonfiction.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　a preference for; suppress; didacticism
incite
incite: standard unit headword
#307 SE
The author takes issue with the ideological blinders that have distorted much migration research, especially __________ modernization theorists and others for their untested assumptions of an immobile preindustrial past.The author takes issue with the ideological blinders that have distorted much migration research, especially __________ modernization theorists and others for their untested assumptions of an immobile preindustrial past.
答案：C, D　castigating; chastising
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#308 TC TRIPLE
Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky's description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky's description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; accurate; impugned
countenance
countenance: standard unit headword
#309 SE
Since the data we have analyzed are so __________, it would be hazardous to draw a definite conclusion.Since the data we have analyzed are so __________, it would be hazardous to draw a definite conclusion.
答案：C, D　meager; paltry
copious
copious: standard unit headword
#310 TC TRIPLE
Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animal's energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)__________ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)__________ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)__________ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animal's energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)__________ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)__________ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)__________ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　onset; put up with constant hunger; mimic
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#311 SE
Sports stars are often __________ figures, regarded as representative of the city or country for which they compete.Sports stars are often __________ figures, regarded as representative of the city or country for which they compete.
答案：A, F　totemic; emblematic
protean
protean: standard unit headword
#312 TC TRIPLE
The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize
remedy
remedy: standard unit headword
#313 SE
In Ramachandran’s opinion, it is perfectly acceptable to propose bold speculations about the brain, even if these speculations seem __________; as Ramachandran frequently remarks, science thrives on risky conjecture.In Ramachandran’s opinion, it is perfectly acceptable to propose bold speculations about the brain, even if these speculations seem __________; as Ramachandran frequently remarks, science thrives on risky conjecture.
答案：A, F　unfounded; baseless
verifiable
verifiable: standard unit headword
#314 TC TRIPLE
An invasive creeping weed native to the Mediterranean region, Cirsium arvense has been present in Yellowstone National Park at least since the nineteenth century. Because of its extensive root system, the plant defies attempts to (i)__________ it by normal methods, and consequently, park officials have (ii)__________ efforts to do so. Warming temperatures have also (iii)__________ of the thistle's fortune.An invasive creeping weed native to the Mediterranean region, Cirsium arvense has been present in Yellowstone National Park at least since the nineteenth century. Because of its extensive root system, the plant defies attempts to (i)__________ it by normal methods, and consequently, park officials have (ii)__________ efforts to do so. Warming temperatures have also (iii)__________ of the thistle's fortune.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　control; largely abandoned; helped
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#315 SE
In order to cultivate new repertoire, the music industry is providing a hearing for previously __________ composers.In order to cultivate new repertoire, the music industry is providing a hearing for previously __________ composers.
答案：E, F　anonymous; obscure
thwart
thwart: standard unit headword
#316 TC TRIPLE
The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollection is (i)__________. One memory can seem (ii)__________ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)__________ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine
elusive
elusive: standard unit headword
#317 SE
In her career as an editor, she pruned and shaped many a writer’s __________ prose into crisp lucidity.In her career as an editor, she pruned and shaped many a writer’s __________ prose into crisp lucidity.
答案：A, C　wayward; errant
incisive
incisive: standard unit headword
#318 TC TRIPLE
Dirac may have (i)__________ the company of other theoretical physicists, but he often (ii)__________, rarely (iii)__________ group discussions and almost never collaborating with others in his own work.Dirac may have (i)__________ the company of other theoretical physicists, but he often (ii)__________, rarely (iii)__________ group discussions and almost never collaborating with others in his own work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　appreciated; kept them at a distance; contributing to

insight: standard unit headword

In order to cultivate new repertoire, the music industry is providing a hearing for previously __________ female composers.In order to cultivate new repertoire, the music industry is providing a hearing for previously __________ female composers.
答案：E, F　anonymous; obscure

thwart: standard unit headword
#320 TC TRIPLE
Some recent research shows that people tend to (i) __________ advice from (ii) __________ source, even to the point that we are willing to forgive an adviser's poor track record. This can drive those offering advice to (iii) __________ and bodes ill for advisers who try to be honest about gaps in their knowledge.Some recent research shows that people tend to (i) __________ advice from (ii) __________ source, even to the point that we are willing to forgive an adviser's poor track record. This can drive those offering advice to (iii) __________ and bodes ill for advisers who try to be honest about gaps in their knowledge.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　trust; a confident; exaggerate their certainty
candid
candid: standard unit headword
#321 SE
The brain has become, for many people, __________ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.The brain has become, for many people, __________ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.
答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with
detached
detached: standard unit headword
#322 TC TRIPLE
Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work, (i) __________ to propose measures that might (ii) __________ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii) __________, they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work, (i) __________ to propose measures that might (ii) __________ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii) __________, they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　forgoing opportunities; ameliorate; reticence
verify
verify: standard unit headword
#323 SE
Although Wayne claims to recognize that __________ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.Although Wayne claims to recognize that __________ evidence is available to make definitive statements, she offers them nonetheless, arriving at some sweeping generalizations.
答案：C, F　insufficient; meager
finite
finite: standard unit headword
#324 TC TRIPLE
Since her death in 1960, Zora Neale Hurston's literary reputation has (i) __________; Hurston died in obscurity, but she now has the status of a major American author. However, despite her (ii) __________ oeuvre, the (iii) __________ her reputation is based mainly on Their Eyes Were Watching God, her second novel, which has stimulated more scholarly writing than all of Hurston's other books combined.Since her death in 1960, Zora Neale Hurston's literary reputation has (i) __________; Hurston died in obscurity, but she now has the status of a major American author. However, despite her (ii) __________ oeuvre, the (iii) __________ her reputation is based mainly on Their Eyes Were Watching God, her second novel, which has stimulated more scholarly writing than all of Hurston's other books combined.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　undergone a dramatic change; substantial; growth of
undermine
undermine: standard unit headword
#325 SE
The author take issues with the ideological blinders that have distorted much migration research, especially __________ modernization theorists and others for their untested assumptions of an immobile preindustrial past.The author take issues with the ideological blinders that have distorted much migration research, especially __________ modernization theorists and others for their untested assumptions of an immobile preindustrial past.
答案：C, D　castigating; chastising

undermine: standard unit headword
#326 TC TRIPLE
To comprehend the Continental Army of the American Revolutionary War in more (i) __________ terms, Holly A. Mayer would have the general reader (ii) __________ a functioning community consisting not just of rank-and-file soldiers and officers but a whole variety of (iii) __________ noncombatants as well, such as the wives and children of enlistees.To comprehend the Continental Army of the American Revolutionary War in more (i) __________ terms, Holly A. Mayer would have the general reader (ii) __________ a functioning community consisting not just of rank-and-file soldiers and officers but a whole variety of (iii) __________ noncombatants as well, such as the wives and children of enlistees.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　holistic; envision; attendant
detached
detached: standard unit headword
#327 SE
Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.Our mass media are much more fascinated by bad ideas or the failure of good ones than by successes: we drown in bad news—tales of how things went wrong—but we have only the most __________ discussion on how they might go right.
答案：D, F　cursory; perfunctory
incisive
incisive: standard unit headword
#328 TC TRIPLE
There are some biologists, open in principle to using computer modeling in their work, who (i) __________ it because they are (ii) __________ the mathematics such approaches involve. The computer modelers who are their potential collaborators can (iii) __________ by taking a dismissive attitude toward scientists who have relatively little quantitative training.There are some biologists, open in principle to using computer modeling in their work, who (i) __________ it because they are (ii) __________ the mathematics such approaches involve. The computer modelers who are their potential collaborators can (iii) __________ by taking a dismissive attitude toward scientists who have relatively little quantitative training.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　avoid; intimidated by; exacerbate this situation
alleviate
alleviate: standard unit headword
#329 SE
The book brings together many valuable reports on conservation projects, but with less variety than might have been wished: nearly half the contributors are from the same state, and consequently, the case studies are similarly __________ geographically.The book brings together many valuable reports on conservation projects, but with less variety than might have been wished: nearly half the contributors are from the same state, and consequently, the case studies are similarly __________ geographically.
答案：D, F　circumscribed; restricted
polarize
polarize: standard unit headword
#330 TC TRIPLE
The artist's portraits of the social elite, with their luscious sensuality and insistent prettiness, have often been regarded with sly amusement, if not outright (i) __________. One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.The artist's portraits of the social elite, with their luscious sensuality and insistent prettiness, have often been regarded with sly amusement, if not outright (i) __________. One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derision; appositeness; willingness to flatter
candor
candor: standard unit headword
#331 SE
It's hardly __________ the committee calls for: rudimentary competence would be an improvement on the current chaos.It's hardly __________ the committee calls for: rudimentary competence would be an improvement on the current chaos.
答案：D, F　faultlessness; perfection
disarray
disarray: standard unit headword
#332 TC TRIPLE
Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　synonymous with; free from; appropriated by
inimical
inimical: standard unit headword
#333 SE
The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has __________ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has __________ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.
答案：D, F　a contentious; a fraught
curious
curious: standard unit headword
#334 TC TRIPLE
Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　an impersonal; irrelevant to; inseparable from
countenance
countenance: standard unit headword
#335 SE
Hsu is __________ by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently flouted established fashion trends.Hsu is __________ by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently flouted established fashion trends.
答案：A, C　independent; freewheeling
gregarious
gregarious: standard unit headword

The (i)__________ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.The (i)__________ nature of the candidate’s comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as a radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　platitudinous; innocuous; conciliatory

答案：A, E　equivocal; ambiguous

We might be much closer to building the next generation of observatories if competing groups of astronomers had not __________ repeated chances to collaborate; instead, three separate projects with common goals are fighting one another for funding.We might be much closer to building the next generation of observatories if competing groups of astronomers had not __________ repeated chances to collaborate; instead, three separate projects with common goals are fighting one another for funding.
答案：A, D　spurned; rebuffed

#340 TC TRIPLE
The notion of popular science or of the public understanding of science is very much a concern in today's world, where we can see (i) __________ between science and nonscience. But in the nineteenth century, there was still an enviable mixing and cross-fertilization between seemingly (ii) __________ subjects, and there was no (iii) __________ what counted as science, let alone how a public understanding might differ from any other sort of understanding.The notion of popular science or of the public understanding of science is very much a concern in today's world, where we can see (i) __________ between science and nonscience. But in the nineteenth century, there was still an enviable mixing and cross-fertilization between seemingly (ii) __________ subjects, and there was no (iii) __________ what counted as science, let alone how a public understanding might differ from any other sort of understanding.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　a clear demarcation; disparate; consensus on

let alone: standard unit headword | candor: standard unit headword
#341 SE
Although diabetic or extremely large dogs need specific diets, specialist pet foods may not be __________ for ordinary dogs: some veterinary nutritionists now suspect that grain-free diets promote canine heart disease.Although diabetic or extremely large dogs need specific diets, specialist pet foods may not be __________ for ordinary dogs: some veterinary nutritionists now suspect that grain-free diets promote canine heart disease.
答案：C, F　salubrious; optimal

#342 SE
Recent discoveries suggest that mathematical techniques developed by ancient Babylonian astronomers __________ those of medieval European scholars: Babylonian trapezoid procedures can be viewed as a concrete example of graphical methods devised by fourteenth-century philosopher Nicole Oresme.Recent discoveries suggest that mathematical techniques developed by ancient Babylonian astronomers __________ those of medieval European scholars: Babylonian trapezoid procedures can be viewed as a concrete example of graphical methods devised by fourteenth-century philosopher Nicole Oresme.
答案：D, E　anticipate; prefigure

undermine: standard unit headword
#343 SE
Dogs' intake of olfactory information is less __________ than that of humans because dogs exhale through the side slits of their nostrils, keeping a continuous flow of inhaled air in their snouts for smelling.Dogs' intake of olfactory information is less __________ than that of humans because dogs exhale through the side slits of their nostrils, keeping a continuous flow of inhaled air in their snouts for smelling.
答案：C, F　punctuated; intermittent
mediate
mediate: standard unit headword
#344 SE
With representatives on every continent except Antarctica, bats are extraordinarily __________, accounting for one in every five species of mammals alive today.With representatives on every continent except Antarctica, bats are extraordinarily __________, accounting for one in every five species of mammals alive today.
答案：B, F　ubiquitous; widespread

gregarious: standard unit headword

Actors may have swooned over him, but producers, for whom the director lost vast amounts of money during his fifty-year filmmaking career, were less __________.Actors may have swooned over him, but producers, for whom the director lost vast amounts of money during his fifty-year filmmaking career, were less __________.
答案：B, E　enchanted; enthralled
open-minded
open-minded: standard unit headword
#346 SE
The athlete had long been recognized as having two sides to her personality: she was an imperious, steely competitor and at the same time __________ sportswoman.The athlete had long been recognized as having two sides to her personality: she was an imperious, steely competitor and at the same time __________ sportswoman.
答案：A, E　a magnanimous; a generous
vigorous
vigorous: standard unit headword
#347 SE
Nanocrystalline photocatalysts are __________ as removers of hazardous contaminants; thus, expanding their practical applicability by improving their visible light absorption is one of the most important objectives in photocatalytic studies.Nanocrystalline photocatalysts are __________ as removers of hazardous contaminants; thus, expanding their practical applicability by improving their visible light absorption is one of the most important objectives in photocatalytic studies.
答案：B, F　promising; viable
unrivaled
unrivaled: standard unit headword
#348 SE
Primates are, for the most part, very __________: walk through a tropical forest, and it is highly likely that if you encounter any mammals, it will be a primate that you see or hear.Primates are, for the most part, very __________: walk through a tropical forest, and it is highly likely that if you encounter any mammals, it will be a primate that you see or hear.
答案：B, D　noticeable; conspicuous
gregarious
gregarious: standard unit headword

The simplicity of Ann Hamilton's sculpture, a single drop of water snaking its way down a vast white wall, __________ the elaborate technical apparatus hidden beneath its surface.The simplicity of Ann Hamilton's sculpture, a single drop of water snaking its way down a vast white wall, __________ the elaborate technical apparatus hidden beneath its surface.
答案：C, D　belied; contradicted

undermine: standard unit headword

In spite of its outwardly __________ demeanor, Guatemala City was a compelling place to be in 1954.In spite of its outwardly __________ demeanor, Guatemala City was a compelling place to be in 1954.
答案：E, F　workaday; quotidian
detached
detached: standard unit headword
#351 SE
My colleagues __________ the journalist's vaunted expertise at securing high-profile interviews, declaring that, given her family's connections to celebrities, obtaining such interviews must be no more difficult than asking a friend out for coffee.My colleagues __________ the journalist's vaunted expertise at securing high-profile interviews, declaring that, given her family's connections to celebrities, obtaining such interviews must be no more difficult than asking a friend out for coffee.
答案：B, C　disparaged; dismissed
thwart
thwart: standard unit headword
#352 SE
The extraction of ancient DNA from fossilized remains is often __________ because as an organism decomposes, its DNA gradually comes to be replaced by the DNA of the organisms that feed on it.The extraction of ancient DNA from fossilized remains is often __________ because as an organism decomposes, its DNA gradually comes to be replaced by the DNA of the organisms that feed on it.
答案：B, D　arduous; laborious
superfluous
superfluous: standard unit headword
#353 SE
For centuries people used the vocabulary of medicine to think about how our bodies are structured, but the language of distinct organs and systems does not do justice to the __________ nature of the body.For centuries people used the vocabulary of medicine to think about how our bodies are structured, but the language of distinct organs and systems does not do justice to the __________ nature of the body.
答案：D, F　interwoven; integrated
balanced
balanced: standard unit headword
#354 SE
For every __________ reader of Vestiges there were many more who became vocal critics, and the book aroused heated opposition from a broad range of British society.For every __________ reader of Vestiges there were many more who became vocal critics, and the book aroused heated opposition from a broad range of British society.
答案：C, D　sympathetic; well-disposed
